source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/01_HAD1717MC, MG/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/11_Clinical Evaluation/A-CLSP-01 Clinical Literature Searching Protocol.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Protocol for systematic clinical literature searching to verify clinical performance and safety of HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines literature sources, search strategy, selection criteria, appraisal criteria, use of post-market information, and update timing for clinical evaluation documentation.
keyPoints: The protocol aims to verify product performance and safety in clinical terms by systematically searching and analyzing clinical literature.
The searched literature must have evidence for technical equivalence, clinical equivalence, and biological equivalence.
Literature selection is stated to comply with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev. 4.
Post-market surveillance information may include customer satisfaction reports, complaint reports, recall data, and literature.
The protocol should be updated at least every 3 years.

evidence.claim: The document is Report No. A-CLSP-01 Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-CLSP-01 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The revision history lists Rev. 0 dated Sep/05/2019 for establishment.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Rev. date Sep/05/2019 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The protocol applies to digital flat panel X-ray detector models HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector HAD1717MG, HAD1717MC

evidence.claim: The protocol's purpose is to verify clinical performance and safety by systematically searching and analyzing clinical literature.
evidence.evidenceText: This protocol is aimed to verify our product's performance and safety in clinical terms by systematically searching out clinical literature of our product and analyzing such searched literature.

evidence.claim: The protocol identifies ScienceDirect, PubMed, and Google as data sources.
evidence.evidenceText: Sources of DATA http://www.sciencedirect.com https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/ http://www.google.com

evidence.claim: The search strategy considers performance, safety, clinical data, equivalence, and benchmark device relevance.
evidence.evidenceText: We intended to find literature about the performance and safety, performance, clinical data nature, history. We will establish equivalence to the device under evaluation or the relevance of benchmark devices to the clinical evaluation.

evidence.claim: Searched literature must have technical, clinical, and biological equivalence evidence.
evidence.evidenceText: Searched literature must have equivalent evidence like below Technical equivalence Clinical equivalence Biological equivalence

evidence.claim: The first literature elimination used free full text and publication date filters from 2000/01/01 to 2019/09/05.
evidence.evidenceText: Filters activated: Free full text, Publication date from 2000/01/01 to 2019/09/05.

evidence.claim: The value of clinical data is evaluated against scientific validity considerations in MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev4-A6.
evidence.evidenceText: The criteria for evaluating the value of clinical data are based on the case of a trial lacking scientific validity to demonstrate adequate clinical performance and clinical safety in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev4-A6.

evidence.claim: Clinical investigations are expected to be designed, conducted, and reported according to EN ISO 14155 or a comparable standard, local regulations, and the Declaration of Helsinki.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical investigations are generally expected to be designed, conducted and reported in accordance with EN ISO 14155 or to a comparable standard, and in compliance with local regulations and the Declaration of Helsinki.

evidence.claim: The searched literature must comply with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev. 4, June 2016.
evidence.evidenceText: The searched literature must be complied with MEDDEV 2.7.1 (Rev. 4, June 2016) as shown below in the Flow Chart.

evidence.claim: Stage 0 of clinical evaluation defines scope, covered products, technology, conditions of use, intended purpose, and clinical performance or safety claims.
evidence.evidenceText: explains the scope and context of the evaluation, including which products/ models/ sizes/ settings are covered by the clinical evaluation report, the technology on which the medical device is based, the conditions of use and the intended purpose of the device; documents any claims made about the device’s clinical performance or clinical safety.

evidence.claim: Stage 1 identifies pertinent data and explains the literature search strategy.
evidence.evidenceText: Stage 1, identification of pertinent data: - explains the literature search strategy; - presents the nature and extent of the clinical data and relevant pre-clinical data that have been identified.

evidence.claim: Stage 2 appraises pertinent data, including methodology, scientific validity, relevance, weighting, limitations, and rejected data justifications.
evidence.evidenceText: evaluates their methodological quality, scientific validity, the relevance for the evaluation, the weighting attributed to the evidence, and any limitations; presents justifications for rejecting certain data or documents.

evidence.claim: Stage 3 analyzes clinical data for sufficient clinical evidence, benefit-risk profile, consistency, gaps, discrepancies, residual risks, and PMS or PMCF needs.
evidence.evidenceText: identifies residual risks and uncertainties or unanswered questions ... that should be further evaluated during PMS, including in PMCF studies.

evidence.claim: Post-market information may include customer satisfaction reports, complaint reports, recall data, literature, and other brand device data.
evidence.evidenceText: We will gather or survey customer satisfaction report, complain report, recall data of other brand device data, literature or etc.

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co., Ltd. will perform post-market surveillance activities considered during document reviews by the quality manager for PMCF under MEDDEV 2.12-2.
evidence.evidenceText: H&abyz Co., Ltd. will perform post market surveillance activities which will be considered at every documents reviews by quality manager for Post Market Clinical Follow-up(MEDDEV 2.12-2).

evidence.claim: Significant clinical evaluation updates based on post-market experience or scientific publication will be reported to the NB.
evidence.evidenceText: Also related significant update to clinical evaluation which based on post market experience or scientific publication, H&abyz Co., Ltd. will inform to NB at every case.

evidence.claim: The protocol should be updated at least every 3 years.
evidence.evidenceText: This protocol should be updated at least every 3 years to reflect recent publications and technical trends.

evidence.claim: The literature search results are attached to the Clinical Literature Searching Report and Clinical Evaluation Report.
evidence.evidenceText: The literature search results are attached to the Clinical literature searching report and clinical evaluation report.

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit issuing authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text; authoritySignal is general.
The source references NB but does not identify the specific Notified Body.
Manufacturer role is inferred from the statement that H&abyz Co., Ltd. will perform post-market surveillance activities; the source does not provide a formal manufacturer field.
